description: Valxon brand-asset catalog. Reference: brand_manual_valxon.pdf p.8 (silhouettes) + p.9 (composition rules).

fossil-jaw.description: Carnivorous-dinosaur jaw fossil silhouette. Wide horizontal motif — works well as a band along an edge.

fern-leaves.description: Cluster of fern-frond leaves. Compact, organic, several internal shapes — works as a focal motif.

ginkgo-leaf.description: Single ginkgo leaf with stem. Roughly square; the most figurative of the four.

dinosaur-footprint.description: Three-toed theropod footprint silhouette. Vertical motif — the company-mascot reference (Vašek the T-Rex).

rules: Parts of a motif may serve as section dividers — fragments are OK, the whole shape isn't required.
Red motifs (malina/sv-malina) occupy at most 30% of the entire format.
Motifs never overlap, and motifs in the same composition always share a similar scale.
Always used in a format larger than the document/surface they appear on — they function as abstract organic shapes replacing illustrations, never as contained images of themselves.
